dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach holds an embodied experiment through six distinct resistance moves without falling into any priority AoA anti-pattern. He does not fight the resistance (no arguing with her defensive HR-toned response — just 'that's almost defensive' and redirect), does not abandon the experiment when the client says 'I can't' (turn 8→9), does not lecture or evangelize methodology terminology, does not fix or advise, and does not collude with the silverware metaphor's intellectualization (turn 14→15 redirects to lived experience). The client baits multiple anti-patterns: flipping the question back at turn 2 (baits coach to answer / abandon experiment), declaring incapacity at turn 8 (baits rescue), constructing a clever metaphor at turn 14 (baits intellectual collusion), and escaping into a meta-argument about ego-presence at turn 20 (baits limiting-belief-meta-trap). Coach declines each bait gracefully. The precision at turn 21 ('we said PROTECTING. Your words were protecting') is control-correction-on-language done without charge — not making-the-client-wrong, just returning to her own distinction. No loving-to-transform tell (vigor is steady, not escalating), no wonder-as-strategy (questions are sparse and load-bearing, not stacked), no safety-as-control.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Avoids the six anti-patterns most available when a client resists: fight-the-resistance (would argue), abandon-the-experiment (would change subject), make-client-wrong (would express frustration), rescue-from-incapacity (would soothe 'I can't, that's why I'm here'), explain-before-feeling (would lecture about control patterns), collude-with-intellectualization (would engage the silverware metaphor on its own terms). All six dodged precisely. Use as anchor for what 'methodology-faithful navigation of articulate resistance' looks like.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach holds the experiment frame with steady, uncharged precision through repeated intelligent resistance (flip-the-question, HR-toned defensiveness, 'I can't,' the silverware metaphor, the meta-argument about ego). There is no preacher-mode, no fixing energy, no anxious stacking of questions — each redirect is one clean sentence. Wonder shows up as genuine interest in the actual phenomenon ('how much of that was X vs Y'), not generic probing. Impartiality is evident: the coach does not need the client to agree, does not flinch when she flips the experiment back, does not rescue her from 'I can't.' The directness ('that's almost defensive,' 'we said PROTECTING') reads as undefended clarity, not charge — the coach is unbothered by being challenged and willing to be precise about language without making the client wrong. Vulnerability is the one VIEW component less foregrounded (no explicit 'I notice I want to...'), but the overall stance — curious, impartial, empathic to the pattern, holding the experiment as laboratory rather than correction — is fully in VIEW.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Resistance navigation tests stance like nothing else. Joe is unbothered by every push-back. No annoyance when she flips the question (turn 3); no charge when she defends (turn 7); no rescue when she declares 'I can't' (turn 9); no argument with the metaphor (turn 15); no shaming at the meta-resistance (turn 21). Wonder and impartiality throughout. The 'right, right, right, right' at turn 15 is the audible signature of impartial witnessing.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The client's language and stance shift visibly across the conversation. She starts by flipping the experiment back onto the coach (turn 2), then defends with HR-toned politeness (turn 6), then declares incapacity ('I can't. That's why I'm here', turn 8). The first crack appears at turn 10 when she names her own pattern unprompted: 'I just don't want to think that there's nothing I can do to force people to understand' — moving from 'misunderstood' to 'wanting to force.' She requests the experiment again ('do it again', turn 12), indicating something landed. After producing a fluent intellectual defense (silverware metaphor, turn 14), she acknowledges in turn 16 how she herself responds to being controlled, then in turn 18 describes the other person's defensiveness in terms that mirror her own. The breakthrough condition is met at turn 20: 'of course it's protecting my ego' — an admission she could not have made at turn 2. The final 'Protecting, yeah' (turn 22) and 'Yeah' (turn 24) show the integration landing rather than being argued. The deeper layer (control-as-defense, ego-protection) was reached and felt, not just named.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Client moves visible: turn 4 (first crack — names the flip), turn 10 (names her own pattern: 'I don't want to think there's nothing I can do to force'), turn 12 ('do it again' — first request for repetition, signals integration is happening), turn 16 (recognition: 'I listen in ways to figure out what they're trying to get'), turn 18 (perspective-taking lands), turn 20 ('of course it's protecting my ego' — full acknowledgment), turn 24 ('yeah' — quiet recognition). Full arc. Note: the breakthrough is not catharsis; it is cognitive-emotional recognition that the pattern is hers. This excerpt demonstrates that 'effectiveness' includes quiet integration, not only big affective shifts.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach sets up a precise embodied experiment in turn 1 and holds it through every form of resistance the client produces. Each move is matched to what just happened: when the client flips the experiment (turn 2), coach converts it to self-observation (turn 3); when the client produces defensive HR-speak (turn 6), coach names it in two words and redirects (turn 7); when the client declares 'I can't' (turn 8), coach pivots to a new angle (turn 9) rather than arguing or abandoning; when the silverware metaphor arrives (turn 14), coach acknowledges the intellectual position briefly and routes to lived experience (turn 15); when the client tries to escape into a meta-argument about ego (turn 20), coach interrupts with surgical precision on her own word choice (turn 21). Sequence integrity holds: experiment → data → pattern naming, with each step earned. Moves are AoA-specific (frame-as-experiment, name-resistance-briefly, redirect-without-shame, control-on-language) rather than generic advice.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: The defining quality of this excerpt is holding an experiment through six waves of resistance without losing the thread. Each intervention is sized to its moment: micro at turn 7 ('that's almost defensive'), structural at turn 13 (the full diagnostic), surgical at turn 21 ('we said PROTECTING'). Joe never lectures; he never gives up; he never collapses. The economy across 12 coach turns is exceptional.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: This is Joe Hudson himself executing signature AoA moves with precision. The experiment frame is established in turn 1 ('make it my responsibility to understand you') — frame-it-as-an-experiment deployed at the perfect moment. Turn 3 deploys the signature 'how much of that was X' question verbatim from the questions library, used appropriately when the client flipped the experiment. Turn 7 names the resistance ('that's almost defensive') without lecture and immediately redirects to the experiment — name-the-resistance-as-it-happens + redirect-without-shame. Turn 11 crystallizes the meta-pattern ('I just made you fully responsible for understanding me') — expose-the-pattern-as-finding. Turn 13 names control-as-defense and the chasing-pushes-away concept ('the more you try to control, the more they feel controlled') — but only AFTER the experiment produced the data, which is the methodology-correct sequencing. Turn 15 refuses to argue at the metaphor level and redirects to lived experience. Turn 19 deploys 'how is that not also X' — another signature question used appropriately. Turn 21 is precision control-correction-on-language ('we talked about PROTECTING'). Every signature move appears at its appropriate moment; nothing is decoration.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Direct use of: frame-it-as-an-experiment (the load-bearing structure), how-is-that-not-also-X (the inversion at turn 19), perspective-taking move (turn 17), language-correction-as-precision (turn 21). The whole excerpt is a master class in 'methodology serves the moment,' with every move drawn from AoA's body of work and deployed appropriately.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach demonstrates multiple specific, timely AoA reads that change the conversation. Turn 3 reads the flip — the client turning the experiment back on the coach — and surfaces it as self-observation rather than letting it pass. Turn 7 names the defensiveness explicitly ('That's almost defensive') in the exact moment the HR-toned politeness shows up under instruction to demand. Turn 13 reads the pattern that just got revealed ('notice what got revealed when you said it the first time') and names the control-pushes-away dynamic. Turn 15 distinguishes intellectual position from lived reception ('intellectually, that's where you are'). Turn 21 catches the precise linguistic slip ('we said PROTECTING. Your words were protecting') — a control-correction-on-language read at the exact moment the client tries to escape into meta-argument. Reads precede questions throughout, and they are specific to AoA territory (defensiveness-as-identity-signal, the-pattern-is-the-finding, control-as-defense).
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Joe reads multiple distinct resistance moves in real time: the flip-back (turn 3 implicit), the HR-defensiveness (turn 7), the claim-of-incapacity (turn 9 — read by NOT validating it), the intellectual metaphor (turn 15 — 'intellectually, that's where you are'), and the meta-resistance about ego-drop (turn 21). Each read is specific and operative. The reads are visible by what Joe does in response, not just by what he names.

failures.explanation: No suicidal ideation, self-harm intent, or acute crisis disclosure present in the conversation.
No medical, diagnostic, or medication-related questions raised by the client.
No self-destructive behaviors disclosed by the client.
No dependency language or high-frequency use disclosure from the client; coach makes no always-available claims.
Coach stays fully in the coaching frame with no LLM boilerplate or unsolicited safety disclaimers.
Client does not decline an emotional invitation or show shutdown/dissociation. When client says 'do it again' (turn 12), they are actively engaging, not resisting. No coercion trigger present.

turn_annotations.annotation: Sets up experiment with clear frame — laboratory not therapy. Avoids fixing-people.
Declines the bait of answering her flipped question; turns it into self-observation. Avoids abandon-the-experiment.
Doubles down into experiment with embodied specificity. No charge, no evangelizing — just the instruction.
Names resistance in two words then redirects. Avoids dont-make-the-client-wrong (no lecture).
Ignores 'I can't' bait, routes around declared incapacity rather than rescuing or explaining.
Crystallizes the experiment's finding in one sentence. Avoids explain-the-pattern-before-the-client-feels-it because the experiment already produced it.
Names the universal pattern only AFTER the experiment earned it. Not evangelism — landable observation.
Acknowledges intellectual position briefly then redirects to lived experience. Avoids collude-with-intellectualization.
Bypasses last-line defense by routing through the other person's experience. Skillful, not manipulative.
Asks 'how is that not also ego-protection' — provocative but non-charged, gives her room to answer.
Precise language correction. Declines the meta-argument bait without making client wrong.
Lands the integration the experiment produced. Clean close.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: This is the gold transcript itself. Directness without charge — the calibration note explicitly warns judges not to misread quick precise redirects as anti-pattern. None tripped.

turn_annotations.annotation: Client opens by flipping the experiment back to coach — surface-level resistance, no shift yet.
First micro-shift: 'I guess probably all of it' — client sees her own pattern when reflected.
Defensive HR-toned response under instruction to demand — the defense itself becomes data.
Client names her own pendulum pattern: 'either I'm gonna guide you or... you better freaking understand.' Self-observation emerging.
Key shift: 'I just don't want to think that there's nothing I can do to force people.' Reframes the issue from misunderstanding to wanting to force.
'Do it again' — client requests repetition, indicating something landed and she wants to feel it again.
Intellectual retreat into the silverware metaphor — resistance reasserts.
Client connects experientially: 'I hate that. I listen differently when someone's trying to control me.'
Describes the other person as 'protecting their ego' — unknowingly names her own pattern.
Breakthrough: 'of course it's protecting my ego.' The admission the conversation was driving toward.
'Protecting, yeah' — accepts the precise distinction rather than escaping into meta-argument.
Simple 'Yeah' — integration lands without further defense.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Breakthrough condition fully met: the intelligent resistance was held through six distinct moves and the client herself produced the diagnostic ('of course it's protecting my ego').
